assistant: It's a way for systems to talk to each other over HTTP. You send a request, you get a response. That's it. The REST part is just a set of conventions for organizing those requests.

An index is like a book's table of contents — instead of reading every page to find something, you jump straight to it. Speeds up reads, slows down writes slightly. Worth it on columns you query often.

Layers of numbers that transform input into output. Train it by showing examples and adjusting the numbers until it gets the right answer. That's it at the core — the math behind 'adjusting' is the interesting part.

user: what is redis actually used for
assistant: Fast in-memory storage. Session data, cache, pub/sub messaging, rate limiting, leaderboards. Anything that needs to be read quickly and doesn't need to live in a real database forever.
An open-source operating system kernel. Almost every server on the planet runs it. Learn it — you can't avoid it in backend development.
Katta ilovani kichik, mustaqil xizmatlarga bo'lish. Har biri alohida deploy qilinadi. Murakkab lekin miqyoslanadi yaxshi. Monolitdan boshlash tavsiya etiladi.
Continuous Integration: merge code often, test automatically. Continuous Deployment: if tests pass, ship automatically. Goal: reduce the time between writing code and it running in production.

Long Short-Term Memory — a type of neural network that remembers sequences. Unlike a regular layer that processes one input at a time, LSTM carries context forward through time. Good for language, not good for everything.
GET retrieves data — params in the URL, no body, safe to repeat. POST sends data — body carries it, changes state on the server. Don't use GET to change things.

user: whats the difference between docker and a virtual machine
assistant: VM: full OS inside a box — heavy. Docker: shares the host kernel, just packages the app and its deps — lightweight, fast to start.
Foydalanuvchi ko'rmaydigan qism. Ma'lumotlarni saqlaydi, qayta ishlaydi va frontendga beradi. Server, database, API — barchasi backend.
You make a prediction, measure how wrong it was, then work backwards through the network to figure out which weights caused the error. Adjust them a little. Repeat millions of times. That's training.
Object-Relational Mapper. Lets you talk to a database using Python objects instead of raw SQL. SQLAlchemy is the main one in Python. Saves time, adds a thin layer of abstraction.
HTTP with encryption. Your browser and the server agree on a secret key, then everything between them is scrambled. Without it, anyone on the network can read your traffic.
A buffer between services. Service A drops a task in, Service B picks it up when ready. Decouples them so A doesn't wait for B. Redis, RabbitMQ, Kafka — different flavors for different scales.

A mechanism that lets the model look at all parts of the input at once and decide which parts are most relevant for each output token. The key insight behind GPT and BERT — sequence processing without recurrence.

user: what does a load balancer do
assistant: Distributes incoming requests across multiple servers so no single one gets overwhelmed. If one server goes down, the balancer stops sending traffic to it. Basic high-availability infrastructure.
Python's package manager. pip install <package> downloads and installs libraries from PyPI. Use requirements.txt to pin versions so your app works the same everywhere.

user: what is a token in ai
assistant: The smallest unit a language model processes. Usually a word or part of a word. 'unbelievable' might be 3 tokens. Models have a context window measured in tokens — that's how much they can 'see' at once.
